name: ext-cli
description: Command-line argument parsing, help, and shell completion.
docs:
Specrows-based CLI parsing.

- Compile a specrows JSON command spec with ext.cli.specrows.compile.
- Render help with ext.cli.render_help(spec_json_or_specbin, scope_bytes) (empty scope = root).
- Parse argv bytes with ext.cli.parse_specrows(spec_json_bytes, argv_v1_bytes).
- Parse argv bytes with env defaults using ext.cli.parse_specrows_v2(spec_json_bytes, argv_v1_bytes, env_v1_bytes).
- On error, use ext.cli.err_msg / ext.cli.err_hint / ext.cli.err_usage (or ext.cli.err_doc_v2 for a canonical map).
- On success, use ext.cli.matches_cmd and ext.cli.matches_get.

Typed option value kinds:
- STR, PATH, U32, I32, BOOL, ENUM, BYTES, BYTES_HEX

Typed opt encodings (matches_get kind=2):
- STR|PATH|ENUM|BYTES: raw token bytes
- U32|I32: 4-byte little-endian
- BOOL: 1 byte (0 or 1)
- BYTES_HEX: decoded bytes

Error schema (via ext.cli.err_doc_v2):
- { code, message, hint, usage, arg?, scope? }
